mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.5% and remains there for at least one month, the zero lower bound constraint becomes binding, signaling exhausted conventional monetary policy. This regime shift reduces the equity risk premium compression benefit and increases uncertainty about future stimulus effectiveness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS=0.13% indicates zero lower bound regime
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% over any 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC > 0.05 fails to persist across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals deteriorating labor market momentum. This decline reduces household income expectations and consumer confidence, which dampens retail sales and equity valuations over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month PAYEMS declines are followed by positive SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or the correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 20-day forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When real retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below its 12-month moving average while simultaneously housing starts (HOUST) fall below 600 thousand units annualized, aggregate demand weakness is confirmed across both consumer and investment channels. This dual weakness predicts negative equity returns as earnings growth expectations compress over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth < 12-month MA AND HOUST < 600 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:current HOUST=591.0 thousand units near threshold
failure_condition: The joint condition (RSAFS deceleration AND HOUST < 600k) is followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (measured via logistic regression coefficient) is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 1.5% month-over-month, signaling robust supply-side capacity utilization and pricing power. This supply strength supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprrice growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: INDPRO accelerations above 1.5% MoM are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or the average 5-day return differential between high and low INDPRO growth regimes is less than 0.5% (t-test p > 0.20).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, indicating accelerating labor market deterioration. This deterioration signals an imminent recession and triggers equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as investors front-run earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: UNRATE increases exceeding 0.3pp over 3 months are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 45% of instances, or the predictive power (Rank IC) falls below 0.04 over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 2%, a negative real interest rate regime emerges. This regime compresses equity risk premiums initially but eventually triggers Fed tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as duration risk reprices.
regime_hint: negative real rates with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: The joint condition (inflation > 3% YoY AND FEDFUNDS < 2%) is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.20) in a logistic regression model.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized in the most recent quarter, signaling late-cycle demand exhaustion. This deceleration reduces forward earnings growth expectations and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as consensus downgrades propagate.
failure_condition: Quarters with GDPC1 growth below 1.5% annualized are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or the correlation between GDPC1 growth and forward 60-day returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) over a 10-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above 700 thousand units annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously grow above their 6-month moving average, signaling synchronized demand strength across residential and consumer channels. This dual demand acceleration supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as growth expectations expand.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 700k units AND RSAFS growth > 6-month MA
evidence_refs: fact:current HOUST=591.0 thousand units below threshold
failure_condition: The joint condition (HOUST > 700k AND RSAFS > 6-month MA) is followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.20) in a logistic regression framework.
